sourceMovie.title: Amour
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Amour (2012) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Michael Haneke's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: High School Musical 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Amour clicked for you, High School Musical 2 (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Blue Valentine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Amour clicked for you, Blue Valentine (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Amour?
faq.answer: Try High School Musical 2, Blue Valentine, Something's Gotta Give—each shares DNA with Amour while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Amour?
faq.answer: Amour (2012) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Amour worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
